channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Estamos apostando en contra de nuestro futuro
item.description: En Estados Unidos dificultamos la virtud y facilitamos el acceso al vicio, como el juego y la pornografía.
item.dc:creator: David French

item.title: ¿Tenis o pickleball? Te decimos los beneficios de estos deportes
item.description: Con raqueta o pala en mano, cualquiera de estos deportes mejoran tu perspectiva cardiovascular.
item.dc:creator: Holly Burns

media:content.media:credit: Calla Kessler para The New York Times

item.title: La reina del salto de cuerda vive en Beverly Hills y tiene 82 años
item.description: Annie Judis dice que el propósito de su vida es desafiar las expectativas que se tienen sobre la gente de su edad.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Friedman

media:content.media:credit: Cassandra Giraldo for The New York Times

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 18, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Uncertainty Remains at Strait of Hormuz After Reopening Announcement
item.description: President Trump said the waterway was open again but that the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports would continue until a deal was reached. Iran’s top negotiator said the strait would close unless the blockade was lifted.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Para Pedro Sánchez, Trump ha sido un salvavidas político en España
item.description: Para los políticos de izquierda en el mundo, el mandatario español es un héroe por enfrentarse al presidente de EE. UU. En España, la confrontación con Trump es vista como un rescate de los desafíos internos.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: El presidente del Gobierno español, Pedro Sánchez, dirigiéndose al Parlamento en marzo.

item.title: In Angola, Pope Leo XIV Faces the Legacy of Slavery
item.description: His visit includes a trip to a shrine where enslaved Africans were baptized before being forced into the treacherous voyage across the Atlantic Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Cláudio C. Silva and John Eligon

media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV in Cameroon on Thursday. He is scheduled to visit Angola on Saturday as part of his 10-day trip through Africa.

item.title: Trump Spat Gives Spain Leader Pedro Sánchez a Political Lifeline
item.description: To leftists abroad, Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez of Spain is a hero for standing up to President Trump. At home, Mr. Trump is seen as Mr. Sánchez’s political savior from thorny domestic challenges.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez of Spain addressing Parliament in March.

item.title: A Potent Threat in Strait of Hormuz: Iran’s “Mosquito Fleet”
item.description: Separate from the regular Iranian Navy, with boats that often go more than 115 miles per hour, it’s what a retired U.S. official calls a “disruptive force.”
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: An Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps speedboat and navy ship in the Persian Gulf, in 2024.

item.title: How Trump Helped Pope Leo Find His Voice
item.description: After his election last year, Leo XIV developed a reputation as a mild-mannered mediator. President Trump’s attacks have made him more combative.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV in Cameroon on Friday. His strident speeches in Africa this week have marked a shift in his rhetoric.

item.title: Corrections: April 19, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Sunday, April 19, 2026.

item.title: Corrections: April 18, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Saturday, April 18, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Luke Tennie Stirs the Pot
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Saturday, April 18, 2026.

item.title: Federal Court Temporarily Freezes Nexstar’s Merger With Tegna
item.description: The judge said the two television companies could not combine operations while an antitrust lawsuit proceeded. Nexstar said its deal was already done.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin

media:content.media:description: A federal judge temporarily blocked Nexstar from combining its operations with the station group Tegna.

item.title: Trump Extends Sanctions Exemption on Some Russian Oil as High Gas Prices Persist
item.description: The Trump administration made the announcement hours after Iran said that the Strait of Hormuz was open to commercial ships.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said on Wednesday that the U.S. government would not renew the sanctions exemption on Russian oil already at sea.

item.title: Trump Expected to Loosen Restrictions on Psychedelic Drugs
item.description: An upcoming executive order is intended to accelerate research into the compounds’ efficacy in treating PTSD, depression and other mental health problems, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Jacobs

media:content.media:description: Psilocybin doses being prepared at the Numia Healing Center in Denver earlier this year.

item.title: In Phoenix, Trump Eyes Lower Gas Prices and Frets About the Midterms
item.description: President Trump told a crowd of supporters that the price of oil and gas was coming down, part of an effort in recent days to present a rosier picture of the nearly two-month-long Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Trump delivered remarks at a Turning Point event in Phoenix on Friday.

item.title: ‘Wait Just a Sec’
item.description: Jesse Cohn delivers a coldblooded killer of a Saturday puzzle.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:description: One way to improve accuracy: Solve with a little poison dart frog on the eraser end of your pencil.

item.title: Trump Will Participate in a Marathon Bible Reading
item.description: He will read a passage from the Old Testament that his Christian supporters cite as a call to national repentance and divine blessing.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Graham

media:content.media:description: The authorities dispersed protesters in 2020 so President Trump could have his photo taken with a Bible in front of a Washington church.

item.title: Our Tax System Is Bad for America
item.description: Our current tax system isn’t fair, and that is bad for social solidarity, argues the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein. The tax expert Ray Madoff joins him to discuss how a fairer system is a moral necessity.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: Trump’s Dispute With Pope Leo Deepens Divisions on the Right
item.description: Sean Hannity criticized the pope. Tucker Carlson attacked Mr. Hannity. And President Trump suggested ranking MAGA figures: “good, bad, and somewhere in the middle.”
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Trump has sharply criticized the pope after his comments about the war in Iran.

item.title: American Airlines Says It Won’t Merge With United
item.description: American issued the statement after news reports that United Airlines’ chief executive had discussed the idea with the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Niraj Chokshi

media:content.media:description: The U.S. airline industry is dominated by four large carriers, and any deals between them would face significant scrutiny from lawmakers and consumers.

item.title: With Vaccines Widely Popular, Kennedy Changes Tone, but Maybe Not His Plans
item.description: Several moves suggest Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. could revive his campaign to question the safety and effectiveness of the shots after the midterm elections.
item.dc:creator: Sheryl Gay Stolberg, Apoorva Mandavilli and Christina Jewett
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. testifying before the House Committee on Ways and Means on Thursday.

item.title: Displaced Family Returns Home Following Truce Between Israel and Lebanon
item.description: Ghia Hajo, 25, returned to her home in Tyre, a city in southern Lebanon that Israel has bombarded with strikes, following the announcement of a 10-day cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal and Monika Cvorak

item.title: Tornados Reported Across the Midwest as Powerful Storms Slam the Region
item.description: One reported tornado downed trees and damaged cars in Lena, Ill., an official said.
item.dc:creator: Judson Jones, Hannah Ziegler and Amy Graff

item.title: White House and Anthropic Hold ‘Productive’ Meeting, Aiming for a Compromise
item.description: Friday’s meeting at the White House followed the introduction of Anthropic’s powerful new artificial intelligence model, Mythos, which U.S. officials believe could be critical for security.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Sheera Frenkel and Tyler Pager
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Trump administration is seeking to strike a compromise with Anthropic after the Pentagon named the company a supply chain risk.

item.title: DHS Expands Deportation Fleet With High-End Jets
item.description: The contract for five planes doubles the Department of Homeland Security’s fleet of jets to expel immigrants, and includes two Gulfstream planes, according to documents and interviews.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo

media:content.media:description: Deportees boarding a chartered plane at Minneapolis-St. Paul airport in February.

item.title: What Maine Voters Are Thinking About Ahead of 2026’s Senate Election
item.description: Our readers in the state tell us about its high-stakes Senate race.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times, left; Robert F. Bukaty/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Gov. Janet Mills and Graham Platner

item.title: Lawsuit Against Alec Baldwin for ‘Rust’ Shooting Heads Toward Trial
item.description: Although the criminal case against Mr. Baldwin was thrown out, the actor still faces civil cases about the fatal shooting of a cinematographer in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs

media:content.media:description: A judge dismissed a criminal case against Alec Baldwin in 2024 after determining that the prosecution had withheld evidence.

item.title: Tyler Robinson, Man Accused of Killing Charlie Kirk, Wants to Ban Cameras From Court
item.description: Lawyers for the man, Tyler Robinson, have argued that filming the court hearings threatens his right to a fair trial.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Bogel-Burroughs

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Zehbrauskas for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mourners at a memorial outside the headquarters for Turning Point USA, the conservative group Charlie Kirk founded, in September.

item.title: Rescuers Mount a Likely Final Push to Save a Stranded Whale
item.description: Rescue crews mounted a likely final push to save a stranded humpback whale off the coast of Northern Germany on Friday. The large mammal, nicknamed “Timmy,” captivated the nation after it was stranded in shallow waters for weeks.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:description: Off Poel Island in northern Germany on Thursday. A humpback whale has been stranded in the area for almost a month.

item.title: New PEPFAR Data Show Worrying Declines in Testing and Treatment for H.I.V.
item.description: The numbers are the first to quantify the effect of the Trump administration’s shutdown and restarting of a program that has saved millions of lives worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: President's Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief

media:content.media:description: A woman held H.I.V. medication that she received through funding from the U.S. President’s Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief, or PEPFAR, along with her hospital records book, at her home in Harare, Zimbabwe, last year.

item.title: Activists Urge Boycott of ‘Bezos Met Gala’ With Posters Around NYC
item.description: A guerrilla activist group is papering the city with posters criticizing the billionaire Jeff Bezos’ involvement in the event, a fund-raiser for the Metropolitan Museum.
item.dc:creator: Alisha Haridasani Gupta

item.title: Mississippi Liquor Stores Run Dry Amid State Distribution Delays
item.description: The state liquor agency has been struggling to make timely deliveries to restaurants, bars and liquor stores since it got rid of outdated conveyor belts at a warehouse in January.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levenson

media:content.media:description: Bare shelves at Spillway Wine and Spirits in Brandon, Miss., on Thursday. Technical issues with the replacement of an aging conveyor system have contributed to prolonged delays in liquor deliveries by the state.

item.title: Cerebras, an A.I. Chip Maker, Files to Go Public as Tech Offerings Ramp Up
item.description: The Silicon Valley chip maker filed a prospectus just as SpaceX, Anthropic and OpenAI prepared for their own listings, in what is shaping up to be a wave of enormous initial public offerings.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz

media:content.media:credit: Christie Hemm Klok for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Cerebras makes specialized computer chips for building artificial intelligence technologies and delivering them to businesses and consumers.

item.title: Western States Need Water. San Diego Has Extra. Will They Make A Deal?
item.description: San Diego County is shopping a surplus of desalinated seawater to Western states that are facing increasingly urgent drought and short supplies.
item.dc:creator: Scott Dance

media:content.media:credit: John Francis Peters for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A view of San Diego and the Pacific Ocean from Cabrillo National Monument.

item.title: Sazerac Prepares $15 Billion Cash Offer for Maker of Jack Daniel’s
item.description: The Kentucky spirits company’s bid for Brown-Forman comes as the industry is being buffeted by a decline in alcohol consumption.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch

media:content.media:description: The potential all-cash bid for Brown-Forman, the maker of Jack Daniel’s, comes as it is already engaged in talks to be acquired by Pernod Ricard of France.

item.title: F.B.I. Launches Manhunt for Youth Volleyball Coach Accused of Sex Abuse
item.description: The bureau has informed high schools, colleges and volleyball clubs in the New York City area that the coach, Edgar Lazaro Castillo, is being investigated in connection with sexual assault.
item.dc:creator: David Waldstein, Neil Vigdor, Andy Newman and Maria Cramer

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Is Declared ‘Open,’ Boosting Hope for a Deal
item.description: Also, a college with a cookie house. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:description: An oil tanker sailing through the Strait of Hormuz today.

item.title: Contours of NYC’s Pied-à-Terre Tax Begin to Take Shape
item.description: As state leaders determine how much owners of high-priced second homes in New York City may have to pay, they are also wrestling with opaque L.L.C.-ownership issues.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes, Mihir Zaveri and Grace Ashford

media:content.media:credit: Cindy Schultz for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gov. Kathy Hochul is discussing a potential surcharge tax on second homes that would kick in on properties worth at least $5 million, with the rates increasing with the value of the home.

item.title: Trump Frames Iran War as All but Over in Optimistic Social Media Flurry
item.description: Iranian officials did not confirm most of Mr. Trump’s claims and disputed several of them.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Trump during an event in Las Vegas on Thursday.

item.title: Amid Conflicting Messages From Trump and Iran, Hopes for Peace Deal Emerge
item.description: On a day when both Iran and the United States declared the Strait of Hormuz opened, hopes for an agreement rose. But statements from President Trump and Iranian leaders about negotiations were sometimes at odds.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi, Peter Eavis, Pranav Baskar and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A rally in support of Iran’s leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, in Tehran on Friday.

item.title: ‘Salaries Are for Suckers’
item.description: How is it possible that a billionaire like Jeff Bezos has a lower tax rate than the average American? The tax expert Ray Madoff joins the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein to explain how the ultra-wealthy pay a disproportionately low amount of federal income taxes in comparison to their overall wealth.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: Kevin Klose, Who Made NPR a Reporting Powerhouse, Dies at 85
item.description: A longtime journalist at The Washington Post, he used his unexpected fund-raising talents to greatly expand the radio organization’s coverage.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

media:content.media:description: Kevin Klose in 2003, when he was president and chief executive of NPR. By 2005, he had boosted the network’s average weekly audience by 50 percent, to 26 million.

item.title: Iris Long, Scientific Mentor to AIDS Activists, Dies at 92
item.description: A chemist, she lent her expertise in drug research to ACT UP, an organization known for its street protests, and helped accelerate the approval of H.I.V. and AIDS treatments.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir

item.title: The Politically Charged Art of Celeste Dupuy-Spencer
item.description: Her vivid paintings examined religious fundamentalism and events like the Jan. 6 riots at the U.S. Capitol. She died this month at 46.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Celeste Dupuy-Spencer, Painter of Hotly Topical Images, Dies at 46
item.description: Her vibrant yet brooding work explored the crises of the Trump era, with paintings depicting the Jan. 6 riots and a mangled Confederate monument.
item.dc:creator: Alex Williams

item.title: Amit Forlit, Accused of Hacking Climate Activists, Appears in Court
item.description: Amit Forlit is accused of running a hacking operation that aimed to thwart climate litigation at the behest of a lobbying firm working for an oil giant.
item.dc:creator: Karen Zraick

media:content.media:credit: Jefferson Siegel for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Federal District Court in New York City. Amit Forlit has been indicted on charges of conspiracy to commit computer hacking, conspiracy to commit wire fraud and wire fraud.

item.title: NYC Doorman Strike Averted After Agreement Is Reached With Building Owners
item.description: The deal would provide pay raises and maintain free health insurance for about 34,000 apartment building workers.
item.dc:creator: Patrick McGeehan

media:content.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The union representing apartment building workers had been negotiating a contract with the owners of about 3,500 buildings.

item.title: Hopes for Peace Deal Rise After Iran Says Strait Is Open
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
